reading.label: B
paragraphs: Omar is a young man. Today he feels very sick. He has a high *fever* and a bad cough. He goes to the *hospital* near his house. At the front desk, a friendly *receptionist* helps him. She takes his name and asks him to wait. Omar sits on a chair as a new *patient*.

Soon, a *nurse* calls his name. She checks his temperature and asks about his pain. Then Omar sees the *doctor*. The doctor listens to his chest and looks at his throat. "You have a small infection," the doctor says. He writes some *medicine* for Omar.

The doctor tells Omar to rest and drink water. He also books an *appointment* for next week. Omar thanks the doctor and the nurse. He feels happy because the *treatment* is easy. Now he wants to be *healthy* again. He goes home to sleep.

questions.question_en: What is the passage mainly about?
questions.explanation_ar: النص يحكي عن مريض يزور المستشفى ليرى الطبيب.

questions.question_en: How does Omar feel at the start of the day?
questions.question_ar: كيف يشعر عمر في بداية اليوم؟
questions.explanation_ar: في البداية يشعر عمر بأنه مريض جداً، عنده حُمّى وسعال.

questions.question_en: Who helps Omar at the front desk?
questions.question_ar: من الذي يساعد عمر عند مكتب الاستقبال؟
questions.explanation_ar: موظف الاستقبال (receptionist) هو من يساعده ويأخذ اسمه.
كلمة fever تعني الحُمّى، أي ارتفاع حرارة الجسم.

questions.question_en: Why is Omar happy at the end?
questions.question_ar: لماذا يشعر عمر بالسعادة في النهاية؟
questions.explanation_ar: عمر سعيد لأن العلاج (treatment) كان سهلاً.

questions.question_en: What will Omar probably do next week?
questions.question_ar: ماذا سيفعل عمر على الأرجح الأسبوع القادم؟
questions.explanation_ar: الطبيب حجز له موعداً (appointment)، فمن المتوقع أن يذهب إليه.

questions.question_en: What happens right after the nurse calls Omar's name?
questions.question_ar: ماذا يحدث مباشرة بعد أن تنادي الممرّضة اسم عمر؟
questions.explanation_ar: بعد أن تناديه الممرّضة، تفحص حرارته وتسأله عن ألمه.

vocab: a sick person who sees a doctor
The patient waits for the doctor.
a place where sick people get help
a person who helps sick people
The doctor looks at his throat.
a person who cares for patients
something you take to feel better
a set time to meet the doctor
He books an appointment for next week.
the care a doctor gives you
He wants to be healthy again.
a person at the front desk

listening.transcript: Hello, I'm Sara. I work as a receptionist at a small hospital. Every morning, many patients come to see the doctor. I write their names and give them a number. I answer the phone and book appointments. When a patient feels worried, I speak in a kind voice. I love my job because I help sick people every day.

exercises.explanation_ar: قالت سارة إنها تعمل في مستشفى صغير.

exercises.question_en: What does Sara give the patients?
What is the talk mainly about?
options: A receptionist's work at a hospital
exercises.explanation_ar: الحديث كله عن عمل موظفة الاستقبال في المستشفى.

exercises.question_en: Why does Sara speak in a kind voice to a worried patient?
options: To help the patient feel calm
exercises.explanation_ar: تتحدث بصوت لطيف لتساعد المريض القلق على الهدوء.

exercises.question_en: Why does Sara love her job?
exercises.explanation_ar: تحب عملها لأنها تساعد المرضى كل يوم.
